questions.text: When the antinodes, in stationary waves, are passing through their equilibrium position, the energy is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: As an object undergoes SHM, such as a mass-spring system or a simple pendulum, its kinetic energy and potential energy continuously transform from one form to the other. At the equilibrium position, where the displacement is zero, the kinetic energy is maximized, as the object reaches its maximum velocity. Simultaneously, the potential energy is at its minimum, as the displacement from the equilibrium position is zero. As the object moves away from the equilibrium, the kinetic energy decreases, reaching zero at the extremum positions, where the potential energy is maximized. The potential energy stored in the system is then converted back into kinetic energy as the object returns towards the equilibrium position

Particles at antinode have maximum velocity and hence maximum kinetic energy while crossing the equilibrium position, while potential energy is zero so the energy is wholly kinetic. questions.text: When a vehicle moving in a straight path suddenly turns, the maximum force will be applied on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Front Wheels: While the front wheels are typically responsible for steering the vehicle into the turn, the maximum force isn't solely concentrated on them. They initiate the change in direction, but the overall force required to redirect the entire mass of the vehicle is distributed. Furthermore, the weight transfer to the outer side means the outer wheels (which could be front or rear depending on the direction of the turn) experience a greater normal force and thus can exert a larger frictional force

Rear Wheels: Similar to the front wheels, the rear wheels contribute to the overall grip and stability during a turn. However, they don't inherently experience the maximum force simply by being at the rear. The weight transfer to the outer side affects both front and rear outer wheels.

On turning the car undergoes circular motion. The inner wheels are the ones closer to the centre of the circle and the outer ones are the ones further away.We assume that R1 is the force acting on the inner wheels and R2 is the force acting on the outer wheels. Both of these forces are exerted by the ground on the wheels and so act upwards. The weight, W of the car acts vertically downwards so it can be said thatR1+R2=WWe may assume that 2a is the distance between the inner and outer wheels, and the height of each wheel is h.

Inner Wheels: The inner wheels are on the inside of the turn. Due to the weight transfer mentioned earlier, the normal force acting on the inner wheels decreases during a turn. With less normal force, the maximum frictional force they can provide is also reduced. Therefore, they experience less force compared to the outer wheels, which are pressed more firmly against the road.

questions.explanation: The maximum force will be applied on the outer wheels.

Thus, when the charge on a capacitor is doubled, the energy stored in the capacitor will be quadrupled (i.e., multiplied by a factor of 4).

This is because the larger cross-sectional area provides more space for the electrons to move, reducing the likelihood of collisions and increasing the flow of current. Therefore, thick wires have less resistance than thin wires and can carry more current without overheating or losing energy due to resistance.

questions.text: Statement:The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze each course of action in response to the given statement:Statement: The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked, and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.Let's evaluate each course of action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.This course of action suggests that offering free breakfast to all students in the primary section may attract many poor students to seek admission at the school. Providing free breakfast could be a positive incentive for economically disadvantaged families to consider enrolling their children in the school.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked, and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.This course of action suggests that the canteen facilities and utensils need to be assessed and upgraded to ensure they can cater to the increased demand for providing free breakfast to all primary students. Proper equipment and facilities are essential for efficiently running the breakfast program.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.This course of action highlights the need for raising funds to sustain the free breakfast program in the long term. Offering free breakfast to all primary students will involve ongoing costs for procuring food items and maintaining the canteen, especially if the school is not receiving any government grant for the initiative.To summarize, the courses of action outline some steps that the school management needs to consider after deciding to provide free breakfast to all primary students. These steps include potentially admitting more students, checking and upgrading the canteen facilities, and raising funds to sustain the program. The success of the initiative will depend on effective planning and resource management by the school management.

Wrong because providing jobs in cities accepts migration instead of solving the cause, and does not reduce rural distress.

questions.explanation: Action I addresses the root cause of the problem (lack of income in villages) and is a sustainable, long-term solution. Action II is a temporary fix that could encourage more migration and further strain urban resources. Therefore, Action I is the more logical course.

questions.text: Statements:I. The literacy rate in the district has been increasing for the last four years.II. The district administration has conducted an extensive training program for the workers involved in the literacy drive.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: If we consider statement I to be the cause, it would mean that the literacy rate is increasing, thus the government is training people for the literacy drive. It does not make any sense this way. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: If we consider statement II to be the cause, it would mean that the government is training people for a literacy drive; hence, when the workers raise awareness among people, the literacy rate in the district increases. This makes a lot more sense. Hence, this option is the correct option.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Statement II is a cause, but Statement I is the effect, not the cause. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: Statement I is an effect, but statement II is a cause, not an effect. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: As the district administration has conducted an extensive training program for the workers involved in the literacy drive, the literacy rate in the district has been increasing for the last four years.
